<div class="sphinxpress-doc">
<section id="markdown-to-epub">
<h1>Markdown to EPUB</h1>
<p>The Markdown workflow renders Markdown to XHTML chapters, packages local image
assets, generates EPUB metadata files, and writes a deterministic ZIP package by
default.</p>
<section id="folder-schema">
<h2>Folder schema</h2>
<p>For general-purpose projects, keep the manuscript in a folder and name files so
sorted filenames match reading order:</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>manuscript/
├── 00-front-matter.md
├── 01-introduction.md
├── 02-chapter.md
└── 99-appendix.md
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The Python helper discovers direct <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">*.md</span></code> children and returns them in filename
order:</p>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">pathlib</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">Path</span>

<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">text2epub</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">BuildOptions</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">create_epub_from_markdown_folder</span>

<span class="n">create_epub_from_markdown_folder</span><span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="n">Path</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;manuscript&quot;</span><span class="p">),</span>
    <span class="n">Path</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;book.epub&quot;</span><span class="p">),</span>
    <span class="n">options</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">BuildOptions</span><span class="p">(</span>
        <span class="n">include_title_page</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="n">include_toc_page</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="n">toc_page_numbers</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="p">),</span>
<span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Use <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">discover_markdown_chapters</span></code> when you want to inspect or customize the
chapter list before building:</p>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">pathlib</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">Path</span>

<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">text2epub</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">EpubMetadata</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">MarkdownBook</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">create_epub_from_markdown</span>
<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">text2epub</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">discover_markdown_chapters</span>

<span class="n">chapters</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">discover_markdown_chapters</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">Path</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;manuscript&quot;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="n">book</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">MarkdownBook</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">metadata</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">EpubMetadata</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">title</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">&quot;Book&quot;</span><span class="p">),</span> <span class="n">chapters</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">chapters</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">create_epub_from_markdown</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">book</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">Path</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;book.epub&quot;</span><span class="p">))</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="inputs">
<h2>Inputs</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">text2epub</span> <span class="pre">markdown</span></code> accepts either one Markdown file or a directory. For
directories, direct <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">*.md</span></code> children are sorted by filename and used as spine
order.</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>text2epub<span class="w"> </span>markdown<span class="w"> </span>manuscript/<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>book.epub<span class="w"> </span>--title<span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Book&quot;</span><span class="w"> </span>--language<span class="w"> </span>en<span class="w"> </span>--title-page<span class="w"> </span>--toc-page<span class="w"> </span>--toc-page-numbers
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The Python folder helper exposes the same default. Set <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">recursive=True</span></code> when you
want matching files below nested folders sorted by relative path.</p>
</section>
<section id="generated-title-and-contents-pages">
<h2>Generated title and contents pages</h2>
<p>The EPUB package always includes the EPUB NAV document for reading-system
navigation. For book-like manuscripts you can also insert reader-visible pages
into the spine:</p>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">text2epub</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">BuildOptions</span>

<span class="n">options</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">BuildOptions</span><span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="n">include_title_page</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">include_toc_page</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">toc_page_numbers</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The generated title page uses metadata such as title, description, creators,
publisher, date, and rights. The generated contents page links to the Markdown
chapters, not to the generated front-matter pages.</p>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">toc_page_numbers=True</span></code> writes CSS using <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">target-counter(attr(href),</span> <span class="pre">page)</span></code>. This
requests automatic page numbers from reading systems that support paged-media
counters. EPUB readers without that support keep the TOC links but omit the page
numbers.</p>
</section>
<section id="front-matter">
<h2>Front matter</h2>
<p>The first Markdown file may include simple YAML-like front matter. Only
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">key:</span> <span class="pre">value</span></code> lines are parsed.</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>---
title: Front Matter Title
language: en
author: Ada Lovelace
publisher: Example Press
description: Short description.
rights: Copyright holder/date.
date: 2026-06-22
<span class="gu">identifier: urn:uuid:example</span>
<span class="gu">---</span>

<span class="gh"># Chapter One</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Explicit metadata passed through the Python API or CLI takes precedence over
front matter.</p>
</section>
<section id="headings">
<h2>Headings</h2>
<p>Headings receive stable IDs generated from heading text:</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gh"># Hello World</span>

<span class="gu">## Hello World</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The rendered IDs become <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">hello-world</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">hello-world-2</span></code>. The first <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">#</span></code> heading
in each file is used as the chapter title when no explicit chapter title is set.</p>
</section>
<section id="images">
<h2>Images</h2>
<p>Local images are copied into <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">OEBPS/Images/</span></code> and chapter image references are
rewritten to relative package paths.</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>![<span class="nt">Cover</span>](<span class="na">cover.png</span>)
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Remote images are rejected by default. Set <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">allow_remote_resources=True</span></code> in
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">BuildOptions</span></code> or pass <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">--allow-remote-resources</span></code> to leave remote image URLs
external. External resources may not be accepted by all stores or reading
systems.</p>
</section>
<section id="links-and-safe-inline-xhtml">
<h2>Links and safe inline XHTML</h2>
<p>Raw HTML in Markdown is escaped by default. Links with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">javascript:</span></code> URLs are rejected.
Other links are escaped during XHTML serialization.</p>
<p>Enable safe inline XHTML when your Markdown text intentionally contains EPUB-safe
phrasing markup, for example from an <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">epub2text</span></code> structured fragment export:</p>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">text2epub</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">BuildOptions</span>

<span class="n">options</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">BuildOptions</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">allow_inline_xhtml</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>text2epub<span class="w"> </span>markdown<span class="w"> </span>manuscript/<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>book.epub<span class="w"> </span>--allow-inline-xhtml
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>With this option, Markdown such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">This</span> <span class="pre">is</span> <span class="pre">&lt;em&gt;important&lt;/em&gt;.</span></code> is emitted as
inline XHTML instead of escaped text. The builder rejects raw block XHTML, scripts,
event handler attributes, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">javascript:</span></code> links. Use Markdown syntax for block
structure and reserve raw XHTML for inline phrasing tags such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;em&gt;</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;strong&gt;</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;span&gt;</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;a&gt;</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;code&gt;</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;sup&gt;</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;sub&gt;</span></code>.</p>
</section>
</section>
</div>
{% endraw %}
